identity.purpose: authorized processing test - public practice datasets (SkyCruncher solver/calibration)
identity.authorization: owner-directed intake per docs/INTAKE_SCRAPER_SPEC.md; sources are publicly published practice datasets whose terms MATCH (attribution-only). Raws kept internal; processed results carry required credit + link-back.

citation.site: AstroPix / Catching the Light — Free Practice Files (Jerry Lodriguss)

use_case.evidence: Verbatim page terms: "Please feel free to download the raw files and practice with them even if you have not bought the book. Also, please feel free to post your processing results with my data on your own web page or to various astrophotography web sites and discussion forums. Please include a link back to this page and credit when you do." Download + practice is explicitly granted; our internal testing/calibration IS the granted 'practice' use, and receipts embed the required credit + link-back. NUANCE: the grant explicitly covers posting processed RESULTS with attribution; it does NOT explicitly authorize redistributing the RAW files themselves, so keep raws internal / results-only downstream.

blockers: none-explicit — attribution REQUIRED as a condition (link back to the page + credit Jerry Lodriguss); not a prohibition, but must propagate into receipts/accreditation

hints.value: lights + darks (8-min exposures) + bias (1/4000s); NO flats
DSLR-on-telescope deep-sky long-exposure light frames (8 x 8-min subs)

citation.notes: DSLR CR2 light frames; AT65Q 420mm f/6.5; ~2.1 arcsec/px. Each ZIP is a set of individual light frames; a single extracted .CR2 is a valid single-light test frame. New regime: DSLR-through-telescope long-exposure deep-sky singles.

use_case.evidence: Parent page states: "The data distributed here is under Creative Commons license, attribution: 'Moana Project'." — the license link resolves to creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ (CC BY 4.0, attribution-only). Attribution-only clearly permits an open-source solo-dev project using files for internal testing/calibration with accreditation embedded in receipts.

blockers: none-explicit — CC BY 4.0 imposes only an attribution condition ('Moana Project'), no NC/ND/redistribution prohibition

hints.value: Dark Sky Observatory (DSO), near Fort Davis, TX, USA
Shared calibration frames (darks/flats/bias) for M45 and Horse Head sets

citation.notes: Mono calibration FITS — a regime the corpus entirely lacks. CC BY 4.0 attribution 'Moana Project'. Land on D:\AstroLogic per K:-drive storage law.

hints.value: Dark Sky Observatory (DSO), near Fort Davis, TX, USA
Horsehead/B33 narrowband + RGB light frames, part 1 of 3, >1000 min total integration, dark sky

citation.notes: Mono + narrowband + long focal length + dark site — all axes the corpus lacks. Part 1 of a 3-part set; a single extracted FITS sub is a valid single-frame test frame. CC BY 4.0 attribution 'Moana Project'.

citation.site: PhotographingSpace.com — Cory Schmitz Kalahari Milky Way (Homework Data)

use_case.evidence: No formal license or usage terms stated on the page. Author openly offers the raw files as a public 'homework' processing exercise and only asks participants to "Share your results" and email processed output to info@photographingspace.com. No grant of redistribution/derivative rights and no prohibition either — silent on our internal-testing/receipt-embedding use. Per protocol, UNCLEAR ⇒ owner decides before pulling.

blockers: none-explicit — no stated license; author requests processed results be shared/emailed to info@photographingspace.com (courtesy, not a condition)

hints.value: Kalahari Basin, South Africa/Namibia border (SOUTHERN HEMISPHERE)

citation.notes: Value = SOUTHERN-HEMISPHERE sky (a hemisphere the corpus lacks) + direct single .CR2 URL (no Drive indirection). Body Canon 5D3; diversity is in sky/lens/FL, not sensor. Single 5D3 CR2 light frame (33.3MB), also present inside the 11x stack zip (fetcher sha-dedups). APPROVAL: UNCLEAR license; OWNER-APPROVED by name 2026-07-17 for internal use — raws kept internal, results carry credit + link-back.

citation.site: Siril official tutorial dataset (M8/M20, ASI2600MC) — hosted by Colmic / astrosurf.com

use_case.verdict: UNCLEAR terms; OWNER-APPROVED 2026-07-17 for internal use
use_case.evidence: NO explicit licensing or attribution terms are stated on the Siril tutorial page or on the reachable archive host. It is the Siril project's official learning dataset (M8/M20, ZWO ASI2600MC on a 106mm refractor), openly linked for tutorial use and widely redistributed, but with no written license grant. Per protocol UNCLEAR ⇒ record and STOP; owner decides before any pull.

blockers: none-explicit — no written license grant on the Siril tutorial page or the archive host

accreditation.organization: Siril project (free-astro.org) — dataset hosted on astrosurf.com/colmic

hints.value: 106mm refractor (Takahashi FSQ-106 Red073x), f/3.7, 388mm focal length
FULL set: 15 lights (180s) + 15 darks (180s) + 15 flats (3s) + 15 bias/offsets (3s)

citation.notes: RVB LIGHT frames: 15 x 180s FITS (zip 553MB → 15 members ~52MB). Directory index enumerated 2026-07-17 (dossier gave only the parent dir URL): www.astrosurf.com/colmic/Traitement_SiriL/brutes/ 301-redirects to colmic.astrosurf.com/Traitement_SiriL/brutes/ (HTTP/legacy-TLS). Raw calibration = 4 RVB zips; the *_resultat.fit/hoo.fit/rvb.fit files on the index are already-PROCESSED tutorial outputs, NOT raw frames. HOO-narrowband variants (Brutes_HOO_180s.zip, Flats_HOO_3s.zip) exist but are SKIPPED (beyond the described full set; ~3GB extra). CALIBRATION GOLD: dedicated color CMOS on a fast refractor WITH a complete calibration stack. APPROVAL: UNCLEAR license; OWNER-APPROVED 2026-07-17 for internal use — raws kept internal, results credit the Siril project.
